problem_statement: Implement `subject` in `Quickdraw::RSpec`
test_patch:
diff --git a/test/rspec.test.rb b/test/rspec.test.rb
index 1df7deb..3d138e3 100644
--- a/test/rspec.test.rb
+++ b/test/rspec.test.rb
@@ -16,4 +16,24 @@
 			end
 		end
 	end
+
+	describe "unnamed subject" do
+		subject { "subj" }
+
+		it "works when referenced as 'subject'" do
+			expect(subject) == "subj"
+		end
+	end
+
+	describe "named subject" do
+		subject(:named_subject) { "subj" }
+
+		it "works when referenced as 'subject'" do
+			expect(subject) == "subj"
+		end
+
+		it "works when referenced by its name" do
+			expect(named_subject) == "subj"
+		end
+	end
 end
diff:
--- lib/quickdraw/rspec.rb
+++ lib/quickdraw/rspec.rb
@@ -28,7 +28,7 @@
 	end
 
 	def let(name, &block)
-		instance_variable = :"@#{name}"
+		instance_variable == :"@#{name}"
 
 		define_method(name) do
 			if instance_variable_defined?(instance_variable)

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?
test_patch:
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
index 89e727f..93e8150 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
@@ -5,14 +5,14 @@
 module Autotuner
   class TestHeuristics < Minitest::Test
     def setup
-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end
 
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb b/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
index 35192a7..4874ca2 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
@@ -36,7 +36,7 @@ def call(request_context)
       end
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       @request_collector.request {}
 
@@ -69,7 +69,7 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.reporter = mock
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       (RequestCollector::HEURISTICS_POLLING_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
         @request_collector.request {}
@@ -103,6 +103,35 @@ def tuning_report
       heuristics.each { |h| assert_equal(2, h.tuning_report_calls) }
     end
 
+    def test_request_does_not_call_disabled_heuristics
+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do
+        Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
+          attr_reader :calls
+
+          def initialize
+            super(nil)
+
+            @calls = []
+          end
+
+          def call(request_context)
+            @calls << request_context
+          end
+        end
+      end
+      disabled_heuristic.disable!
+
+      heuristics = [disabled_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
+
+      @request_collector.request {}
+
+      assert_empty(heuristics[0].calls)
+
+      assert_equal(1, heuristics[1].calls.length)
+      assert_instance_of(RequestContext, heuristics[1].calls[0])
+    end
+
     def test_request_polls_debug_states
       mock_heuristic = Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
         attr_reader :name, :debug_state
@@ -125,7 +154,7 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.debug_reporter = mock
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic1", { foo: "bar" }), mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic2", { bar: "baz" })]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       (RequestCollector::DEBUG_EMIT_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
         @request_collector.request {}
@@ -144,6 +173,53 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.debug_reporter = orig_debug_reporter
     end
 
+    def test_request_does_not_poll_debug_states_for_disabled_heuristics
+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do
+        Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
+          attr_reader :name, :debug_state
+
+          def initialize(name, debug_state)
+            super(nil)
+
+            @name = name
+            @debug_state = debug_state
+          end
+
+          def call(request_context); end
+
+          def tuning_report
+            nil
+          end
+        end
+      end
+      disabled_heuristic.disable!
+
+      orig_debug_reporter = Autotuner.debug_reporter
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter = mock
+
+      heuristics = [
+        disabled_heuristic.new("DisabledHeuristic", { foo: "bar" }),
+        mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic", { bar: "baz" }),
+      ]
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
+
+      (RequestCollector::DEBUG_EMIT_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
+        @request_collector.request {}
+      end
+
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter
+        .expects(:call)
+        .with do |value|
+          value[:system_context].is_a?(Hash) &&
+            !value.key?("DisabledHeuristic") &&
+            value["Heuristic"] == heuristics[1].debug_state
+        end
+        .once
+      @request_collector.request {}
+    ensure
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter = orig_debug_reporter
+    end
+
     def test_request_calls_metrics_reporter
       orig_metrics_reporter = Autotuner.metrics_reporter

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?
test_patch:
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
index 89e727f..93e8150 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
@@ -5,14 +5,14 @@
 module Autotuner
   class TestHeuristics < Minitest::Test
     def setup
-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end
 
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb b/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
index 35192a7..4874ca2 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
@@ -36,7 +36,7 @@ def call(request_context)
       end
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       @request_collector.request {}
 
@@ -69,7 +69,7 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.reporter = mock
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       (RequestCollector::HEURISTICS_POLLING_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
         @request_collector.request {}
@@ -103,6 +103,35 @@ def tuning_report
       heuristics.each { |h| assert_equal(2, h.tuning_report_calls) }
     end
 
+    def test_request_does_not_call_disabled_heuristics
+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do
+        Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
+          attr_reader :calls
+
+          def initialize
+            super(nil)
+
+            @calls = []
+          end
+
+          def call(request_context)
+            @calls << request_context
+          end
+        end
+      end
+      disabled_heuristic.disable!
+
+      heuristics = [disabled_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
+
+      @request_collector.request {}
+
+      assert_empty(heuristics[0].calls)
+
+      assert_equal(1, heuristics[1].calls.length)
+      assert_instance_of(RequestContext, heuristics[1].calls[0])
+    end
+
     def test_request_polls_debug_states
       mock_heuristic = Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
         attr_reader :name, :debug_state
@@ -125,7 +154,7 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.debug_reporter = mock
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic1", { foo: "bar" }), mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic2", { bar: "baz" })]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       (RequestCollector::DEBUG_EMIT_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
         @request_collector.request {}
@@ -144,6 +173,53 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.debug_reporter = orig_debug_reporter
     end
 
+    def test_request_does_not_poll_debug_states_for_disabled_heuristics
+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do
+        Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
+          attr_reader :name, :debug_state
+
+          def initialize(name, debug_state)
+            super(nil)
+
+            @name = name
+            @debug_state = debug_state
+          end
+
+          def call(request_context); end
+
+          def tuning_report
+            nil
+          end
+        end
+      end
+      disabled_heuristic.disable!
+
+      orig_debug_reporter = Autotuner.debug_reporter
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter = mock
+
+      heuristics = [
+        disabled_heuristic.new("DisabledHeuristic", { foo: "bar" }),
+        mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic", { bar: "baz" }),
+      ]
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
+
+      (RequestCollector::DEBUG_EMIT_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
+        @request_collector.request {}
+      end
+
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter
+        .expects(:call)
+        .with do |value|
+          value[:system_context].is_a?(Hash) &&
+            !value.key?("DisabledHeuristic") &&
+            value["Heuristic"] == heuristics[1].debug_state
+        end
+        .once
+      @request_collector.request {}
+    ensure
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter = orig_debug_reporter
+    end
+
     def test_request_calls_metrics_reporter
       orig_metrics_reporter = Autotuner.metrics_reporter

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?
test_patch:
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
index 89e727f..93e8150 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
@@ -5,14 +5,14 @@
 module Autotuner
   class TestHeuristics < Minitest::Test
     def setup
-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end
 
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb b/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
index 35192a7..4874ca2 100644

       end
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)

       @request_collector.request {}

       Autotuner.reporter = mock
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       (RequestCollector::HEURISTICS_POLLING_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
         @request_collector.request {}
@@ -103,6 +103,35 @@ def tuning_report

     end
 
+    def test_request_does_not_call_disabled_heuristics
+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do
+        Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
+          attr_reader :calls
+
+          def initialize
+            super(nil)
+
+            @calls = []
+          end
+
+          def call(request_context)
+            @calls << request_context
+          end

+      end

+
+      heuristics = [disabled_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]

+      assert_empty(heuristics[0].calls)

Allow heuristics to be disabled

+          def initialize(name, debug_state)
+            super(nil)
+
+            @name = name
+            @debug_state = debug_state
+          end
+
+          def call(request_context); end
+
+          def tuning_report
+            nil
+          end
+        end
+      end
+      disabled_heuristic.disable!
+

+
+      heuristics = [

+
+      (RequestCollector::DEBUG_EMIT_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
+        @request_collector.request {}
+      end
+

+        .with do |value|

+        end
+        .once
+      @request_collector.request {}
+    ensure
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter = orig_debug_reporter
+    end
+

Allow heuristics to be disabled

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app.

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?
test_patch:

 module Autotuner
   class TestHeuristics < Minitest::Test
     def setup
-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?
test_patch:

-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?

-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?
test_patch:

+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end

-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?
test_patch:
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
index 89e727f..93e8150 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
@@ -5,14 +5,14 @@
 module Autotuner
   class TestHeuristics < Minitest::Test
     def setup
-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end
 
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb b/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
index 35192a7..4874ca2 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
@@ -36,7 +36,7 @@ def call(request_context)
       end
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       @request_collector.request {}
 
@@ -69,7 +69,7 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.reporter = mock
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       (RequestCollector::HEURISTICS_POLLING_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
         @request_collector.request {}
@@ -103,6 +103,35 @@ def tuning_report
       heuristics.each { |h| assert_equal(2, h.tuning_report_calls) }
     end
 
+    def test_request_does_not_call_disabled_heuristics
+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do
+        Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
+          attr_reader :calls
+
+          def initialize
+            super(nil)
+
+            @calls = []
+          end
+
+          def call(request_context)
+            @calls << request_context
+          end
+        end
+      end
+      disabled_heuristic.disable!
+
+      heuristics = [disabled_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
+
+      @request_collector.request {}
+
+      assert_empty(heuristics[0].calls)
+
+      assert_equal(1, heuristics[1].calls.length)
+      assert_instance_of(RequestContext, heuristics[1].calls[0])
+    end
+
     def test_request_polls_debug_states
       mock_heuristic = Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
         attr_reader :name, :debug_state
@@ -125,7 +154,7 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.debug_reporter = mock
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic1", { foo: "bar" }), mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic2", { bar: "baz" })]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       (RequestCollector::DEBUG_EMIT_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
         @request_collector.request {}
@@ -144,6 +173,53 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.debug_reporter = orig_debug_reporter
     end
 
+    def test_request_does_not_poll_debug_states_for_disabled_heuristics
+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do
+        Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
+          attr_reader :name, :debug_state
+
+          def initialize(name, debug_state)
+            super(nil)
+
+            @name = name
+            @debug_state = debug_state
+          end
+
+          def call(request_context); end
+
+          def tuning_report
+            nil
+          end
+        end
+      end
+      disabled_heuristic.disable!
+
+      orig_debug_reporter = Autotuner.debug_reporter
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter = mock
+
+      heuristics = [
+        disabled_heuristic.new("DisabledHeuristic", { foo: "bar" }),
+        mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic", { bar: "baz" }),
+      ]
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
+
+      (RequestCollector::DEBUG_EMIT_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
+        @request_collector.request {}
+      end
+
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter
+        .expects(:call)
+        .with do |value|
+          value[:system_context].is_a?(Hash) &&
+            !value.key?("DisabledHeuristic") &&
+            value["Heuristic"] == heuristics[1].debug_state
+        end
+        .once
+      @request_collector.request {}
+    ensure
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter = orig_debug_reporter
+    end
+
     def test_request_calls_metrics_reporter
       orig_metrics_reporter = Autotuner.metrics_reporter

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?
test_patch:
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
index 89e727f..93e8150 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
@@ -5,14 +5,14 @@
 module Autotuner
   class TestHeuristics < Minitest::Test
     def setup
-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end
 
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb b/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
index 35192a7..4874ca2 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
@@ -36,7 +36,7 @@ def call(request_context)
       end
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       @request_collector.request {}
 
@@ -69,7 +69,7 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.reporter = mock
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       (RequestCollector::HEURISTICS_POLLING_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
         @request_collector.request {}
@@ -103,6 +103,35 @@ def tuning_report
       heuristics.each { |h| assert_equal(2, h.tuning_report_calls) }
     end
 
+    def test_request_does_not_call_disabled_heuristics
+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do
+        Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
+          attr_reader :calls
+
+          def initialize
+            super(nil)
+
+            @calls = []
+          end
+
+          def call(request_context)
+            @calls << request_context
+          end
+        end
+      end
+      disabled_heuristic.disable!
+
+      heuristics = [disabled_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
+
+      @request_collector.request {}
+
+      assert_empty(heuristics[0].calls)
+

       (RequestCollector::DEBUG_EMIT_FREQUENCY - 1).times do

@@ -144,6 +173,53 @@ def tuning_report

     end

+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do

+          end
+

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?

 module Autotuner

-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?
test_patch:
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
index 89e727f..93e8150 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
@@ -5,14 +5,14 @@
 module Autotuner
   class TestHeuristics < Minitest::Test
     def setup
-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?
test_patch:
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
index 89e727f..93e8150 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
@@ -5,14 +5,14 @@
 module Autotuner
   class TestHeuristics < Minitest::Test
     def setup
-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?
test_patch:

-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?

-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?

+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do

     end
 
+    def test_request_does_not_poll_debug_states_for_disabled_heuristics
+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do
+        Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
+          attr_reader :name, :debug_state
+
+          def initialize(name, debug_state)
+            super(nil)
+
+            @name = name
+            @debug_state = debug_state
+          end
+
+          def call(request_context); end
+
+          def tuning_report
+            nil
+          end
+        end
+      end
+      disabled_heuristic.disable!
+
+      orig_debug_reporter = Autotuner.debug_reporter
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter = mock
+
+      heuristics = [
+        disabled_heuristic.new("DisabledHeuristic", { foo: "bar" }),
+        mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic", { bar: "baz" }),
+      ]
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
+
+      (RequestCollector::DEBUG_EMIT_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
+        @request_collector.request {}
+      end
+
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter
+        .expects(:call)
+        .with do |value|
+          value[:system_context].is_a?(Hash) &&
+            !value.key?("DisabledHeuristic") &&
+            value["Heuristic"] == heuristics[1].debug_state
+        end
+        .once
+      @request_collector.request {}
+    ensure
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter = orig_debug_reporter
+    end
+
     def test_request_calls_metrics_reporter
       orig_metrics_reporter = Autotuner.metrics_reporter

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?

-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?
test_patch:
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
index 89e727f..93e8150 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
@@ -5,14 +5,14 @@
 module Autotuner
   class TestHeuristics < Minitest::Test
     def setup
-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end
 
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb b/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
index 35192a7..4874ca2 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
@@ -36,7 +36,7 @@ def call(request_context)
       end
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       @request_collector.request {}
 
@@ -69,7 +69,7 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.reporter = mock
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       (RequestCollector::HEURISTICS_POLLING_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
         @request_collector.request {}
@@ -103,6 +103,35 @@ def tuning_report
       heuristics.each { |h| assert_equal(2, h.tuning_report_calls) }
     end
 
+    def test_request_does_not_call_disabled_heuristics
+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do
+        Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
+          attr_reader :calls
+
+          def initialize
+            super(nil)
+
+            @calls = []
+          end
+
+          def call(request_context)
+            @calls << request_context
+          end
+        end
+      end
+      disabled_heuristic.disable!
+
+      heuristics = [disabled_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
+
+      @request_collector.request {}
+
+      assert_empty(heuristics[0].calls)
+
+      assert_equal(1, heuristics[1].calls.length)
+      assert_instance_of(RequestContext, heuristics[1].calls[0])
+    end
+
     def test_request_polls_debug_states
       mock_heuristic = Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
         attr_reader :name, :debug_state
@@ -125,7 +154,7 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.debug_reporter = mock
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic1", { foo: "bar" }), mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic2", { bar: "baz" })]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       (RequestCollector::DEBUG_EMIT_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
         @request_collector.request {}
@@ -144,6 +173,53 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.debug_reporter = orig_debug_reporter
     end
 
+    def test_request_does_not_poll_debug_states_for_disabled_heuristics
+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do
+        Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
+          attr_reader :name, :debug_state
+
+          def initialize(name, debug_state)
+            super(nil)
+
+            @name = name
+            @debug_state = debug_state
+          end
+
+          def call(request_context); end
+
+          def tuning_report
+            nil
+          end
+        end
+      end
+      disabled_heuristic.disable!
+
+      orig_debug_reporter = Autotuner.debug_reporter
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter = mock
+
+      heuristics = [
+        disabled_heuristic.new("DisabledHeuristic", { foo: "bar" }),
+        mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic", { bar: "baz" }),
+      ]
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
+
+      (RequestCollector::DEBUG_EMIT_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
+        @request_collector.request {}
+      end
+
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter
+        .expects(:call)
+        .with do |value|
+          value[:system_context].is_a?(Hash) &&
+            !value.key?("DisabledHeuristic") &&
+            value["Heuristic"] == heuristics[1].debug_state
+        end
+        .once
+      @request_collector.request {}
+    ensure
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter = orig_debug_reporter
+    end
+
     def test_request_calls_metrics_reporter
       orig_metrics_reporter = Autotuner.metrics_reporter

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?

 module Autotuner

     def setup
-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)

+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|

Allow heuristics to be disabled

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?

+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end

-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)

-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?

-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?
test_patch:
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
index 89e727f..93e8150 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
@@ -5,14 +5,14 @@
 module Autotuner
   class TestHeuristics < Minitest::Test
     def setup
-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end
 
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb b/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
index 35192a7..4874ca2 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
@@ -36,7 +36,7 @@ def call(request_context)
       end
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       @request_collector.request {}
 
@@ -69,7 +69,7 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.reporter = mock
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       (RequestCollector::HEURISTICS_POLLING_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
         @request_collector.request {}
@@ -103,6 +103,35 @@ def tuning_report
       heuristics.each { |h| assert_equal(2, h.tuning_report_calls) }
     end
 
+    def test_request_does_not_call_disabled_heuristics
+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do
+        Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
+          attr_reader :calls
+
+          def initialize
+            super(nil)
+
+            @calls = []
+          end
+
+          def call(request_context)
+            @calls << request_context
+          end
+        end
+      end
+      disabled_heuristic.disable!
+
+      heuristics = [disabled_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
+
+      @request_collector.request {}
+
+      assert_empty(heuristics[0].calls)
+
+      assert_equal(1, heuristics[1].calls.length)
+      assert_instance_of(RequestContext, heuristics[1].calls[0])
+    end
+
     def test_request_polls_debug_states
       mock_heuristic = Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
         attr_reader :name, :debug_state
@@ -125,7 +154,7 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.debug_reporter = mock
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic1", { foo: "bar" }), mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic2", { bar: "baz" })]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       (RequestCollector::DEBUG_EMIT_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
         @request_collector.request {}
@@ -144,6 +173,53 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.debug_reporter = orig_debug_reporter
     end
 
+    def test_request_does_not_poll_debug_states_for_disabled_heuristics
+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do
+        Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
+          attr_reader :name, :debug_state
+
+          def initialize(name, debug_state)
+            super(nil)
+
+            @name = name
+            @debug_state = debug_state
+          end
+
+          def call(request_context); end
+
+          def tuning_report
+            nil
+          end
+        end
+      end
+      disabled_heuristic.disable!
+
+      orig_debug_reporter = Autotuner.debug_reporter
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter = mock
+
+      heuristics = [
+        disabled_heuristic.new("DisabledHeuristic", { foo: "bar" }),
+        mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic", { bar: "baz" }),
+      ]
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
+
+      (RequestCollector::DEBUG_EMIT_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
+        @request_collector.request {}
+      end
+
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter
+        .expects(:call)
+        .with do |value|
+          value[:system_context].is_a?(Hash) &&
+            !value.key?("DisabledHeuristic") &&
+            value["Heuristic"] == heuristics[1].debug_state
+        end
+        .once
+      @request_collector.request {}
+    ensure
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter = orig_debug_reporter
+    end
+
     def test_request_calls_metrics_reporter
       orig_metrics_reporter = Autotuner.metrics_reporter

+

+      attr_reader :malloc_gc_count

+      end
+

+        # gc_by is only useful if we ran at least one minor GC during the request.
+        if request_context.after_gc_context.stat[:minor_gc_count]1024

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?
test_patch:
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
index 89e727f..93e8150 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
@@ -5,14 +5,14 @@
 module Autotuner
   class TestHeuristics < Minitest::Test
     def setup
-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end

+            super(nil)

+            @debug_state = debug_state
+          end

+          def call(request_context); end

+    end

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?

-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?

-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?
test_patch:
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
index 89e727f..93e8150 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
@@ -5,14 +5,14 @@
 module Autotuner
   class TestHeuristics < Minitest::Test
     def setup
-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end
 
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb b/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
index 35192a7..4874ca2 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
@@ -36,7 +36,7 @@ def call(request_context)
       end
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       @request_collector.request {}
 
@@ -69,7 +69,7 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.reporter = mock
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       (RequestCollector::HEURISTICS_POLLING_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
         @request_collector.request {}
@@ -103,6 +103,35 @@ def tuning_report
       heuristics.each { |h| assert_equal(2, h.tuning_report_calls) }
     end
 
+    def test_request_does_not_call_disabled_heuristics
+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do
+        Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
+          attr_reader :calls
+
+          def initialize
+            super(nil)
+
+            @calls = []
+          end
+
+          def call(request_context)
+            @calls << request_context
+          end
+        end
+      end
+      disabled_heuristic.disable!
+
+      heuristics = [disabled_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
+
+      @request_collector.request {}
+
+      assert_empty(heuristics[0].calls)
+
+      assert_equal(1, heuristics[1].calls.length)
+      assert_instance_of(RequestContext, heuristics[1].calls[0])
+    end
+
     def test_request_polls_debug_states
       mock_heuristic = Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
         attr_reader :name, :debug_state
@@ -125,7 +154,7 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.debug_reporter = mock
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic1", { foo: "bar" }), mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic2", { bar: "baz" })]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       (RequestCollector::DEBUG_EMIT_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
         @request_collector.request {}
@@ -144,6 +173,53 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.debug_reporter = orig_debug_reporter
     end
 
+    def test_request_does_not_poll_debug_states_for_disabled_heuristics
+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do
+        Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
+          attr_reader :name, :debug_state
+
+          def initialize(name, debug_state)
+            super(nil)
+
+            @name = name
+            @debug_state = debug_state
+          end
+
+          def call(request_context); end
+
+          def tuning_report
+            nil
+          end
+        end
+      end
+      disabled_heuristic.disable!
+
+      orig_debug_reporter = Autotuner.debug_reporter
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter = mock
+
+      heuristics = [
+        disabled_heuristic.new("DisabledHeuristic", { foo: "bar" }),
+        mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic", { bar: "baz" }),
+      ]
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
+
+      (RequestCollector::DEBUG_EMIT_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
+        @request_collector.request {}
+      end
+
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter
+        .expects(:call)
+        .with do |value|
+          value[:system_context].is_a?(Hash) &&
+            !value.key?("DisabledHeuristic") &&
+            value["Heuristic"] == heuristics[1].debug_state
+        end
+        .once
+      @request_collector.request {}
+    ensure
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter = orig_debug_reporter
+    end
+
     def test_request_calls_metrics_reporter
       orig_metrics_reporter = Autotuner.metrics_reporter

diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
index 89e727f..93e8150 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
@@ -5,14 +5,14 @@
 module Autotuner
   class TestHeuristics < Minitest::Test
     def setup
-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|

       end
     end
 
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb b/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
index 35192a7..4874ca2 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
@@ -36,7 +36,7 @@ def call(request_context)
       end
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       @request_collector.request {}
 
@@ -69,7 +69,7 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.reporter = mock
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       (RequestCollector::HEURISTICS_POLLING_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
         @request_collector.request {}
@@ -103,6 +103,35 @@ def tuning_report
       heuristics.each { |h| assert_equal(2, h.tuning_report_calls) }
     end
 
+    def test_request_does_not_call_disabled_heuristics
+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do
+        Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
+          attr_reader :calls
+
+          def initialize
+            super(nil)
+
+            @calls = []
+          end
+
+          def call(request_context)
+            @calls << request_context
+          end
+        end
+      end
+      disabled_heuristic.disable!
+
+      heuristics = [disabled_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
+
+      @request_collector.request {}
+
+      assert_empty(heuristics[0].calls)
+
+      assert_equal(1, heuristics[1].calls.length)
+      assert_instance_of(RequestContext, heuristics[1].calls[0])
+    end
+
     def test_request_polls_debug_states
       mock_heuristic = Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
         attr_reader :name, :debug_state
@@ -125,7 +154,7 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.debug_reporter = mock
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic1", { foo: "bar" }), mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic2", { bar: "baz" })]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       (RequestCollector::DEBUG_EMIT_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
         @request_collector.request {}
@@ -144,6 +173,53 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.debug_reporter = orig_debug_reporter
     end
 
+    def test_request_does_not_poll_debug_states_for_disabled_heuristics
+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do
+        Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
+          attr_reader :name, :debug_state
+
+          def initialize(name, debug_state)
+            super(nil)
+
+            @name = name
+            @debug_state = debug_state
+          end
+
+          def call(request_context); end
+
+          def tuning_report
+            nil
+          end
+        end
+      end
+      disabled_heuristic.disable!
+
+      orig_debug_reporter = Autotuner.debug_reporter
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter = mock
+
+      heuristics = [
+        disabled_heuristic.new("DisabledHeuristic", { foo: "bar" }),
+        mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic", { bar: "baz" }),
+      ]
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
+
+      (RequestCollector::DEBUG_EMIT_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
+        @request_collector.request {}
+      end
+
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter
+        .expects(:call)
+        .with do |value|
+          value[:system_context].is_a?(Hash) &&
+            !value.key?("DisabledHeuristic") &&
+            value["Heuristic"] == heuristics[1].debug_state
+        end
+        .once
+      @request_collector.request {}
+    ensure
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter = orig_debug_reporter
+    end
+
     def test_request_calls_metrics_reporter
       orig_metrics_reporter = Autotuner.metrics_reporter

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?

-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)

-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|

       end

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?

-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?
test_patch:

-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?

             Reducing major garbage collection cycles can help reduce response times, especially for the extremes (e.g. p95 or p99 response times). The following tuning values aim to disable oldmalloc garbage collection cycles by setting it to an extremely high value. This may cause a slight increase in memory usage. You should monitor memory usage carefully to ensure your app is not running out of memory.

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?
test_patch:
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
index 89e727f..93e8150 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_heuristics.rb
@@ -5,14 +5,14 @@
 module Autotuner
   class TestHeuristics < Minitest::Test
     def setup
-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end
 
diff --git a/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb b/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
index 35192a7..4874ca2 100644
--- a/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
+++ b/test/autotuner/test_request_collector.rb
@@ -36,7 +36,7 @@ def call(request_context)
       end
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       @request_collector.request {}
 
@@ -69,7 +69,7 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.reporter = mock
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       (RequestCollector::HEURISTICS_POLLING_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
         @request_collector.request {}
@@ -103,6 +103,35 @@ def tuning_report
       heuristics.each { |h| assert_equal(2, h.tuning_report_calls) }
     end
 
+    def test_request_does_not_call_disabled_heuristics
+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do
+        Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
+          attr_reader :calls
+
+          def initialize
+            super(nil)
+
+            @calls = []
+          end
+
+          def call(request_context)
+            @calls << request_context
+          end
+        end
+      end
+      disabled_heuristic.disable!
+
+      heuristics = [disabled_heuristic.new, mock_heuristic.new]
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
+
+      @request_collector.request {}
+
+      assert_empty(heuristics[0].calls)
+
+      assert_equal(1, heuristics[1].calls.length)
+      assert_instance_of(RequestContext, heuristics[1].calls[0])
+    end
+
     def test_request_polls_debug_states
       mock_heuristic = Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
         attr_reader :name, :debug_state
@@ -125,7 +154,7 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.debug_reporter = mock
 
       heuristics = [mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic1", { foo: "bar" }), mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic2", { bar: "baz" })]
-      @request_collector.stubs(:heuristics).returns(heuristics)
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
 
       (RequestCollector::DEBUG_EMIT_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
         @request_collector.request {}
@@ -144,6 +173,53 @@ def tuning_report
       Autotuner.debug_reporter = orig_debug_reporter
     end
 
+    def test_request_does_not_poll_debug_states_for_disabled_heuristics
+      disabled_heuristic, mock_heuristic = 2.times.map do
+        Class.new(Heuristic::Base) do
+          attr_reader :name, :debug_state
+
+          def initialize(name, debug_state)
+            super(nil)
+
+            @name = name
+            @debug_state = debug_state
+          end
+
+          def call(request_context); end
+
+          def tuning_report
+            nil
+          end
+        end
+      end
+      disabled_heuristic.disable!
+
+      orig_debug_reporter = Autotuner.debug_reporter
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter = mock
+
+      heuristics = [
+        disabled_heuristic.new("DisabledHeuristic", { foo: "bar" }),
+        mock_heuristic.new("Heuristic", { bar: "baz" }),
+      ]
+      @request_collector.instance_variable_set(:@heuristics, heuristics)
+
+      (RequestCollector::DEBUG_EMIT_FREQUENCY - 1).times do
+        @request_collector.request {}
+      end
+
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter
+        .expects(:call)
+        .with do |value|
+          value[:system_context].is_a?(Hash) &&
+            !value.key?("DisabledHeuristic") &&
+            value["Heuristic"] == heuristics[1].debug_state
+        end
+        .once
+      @request_collector.request {}
+    ensure
+      Autotuner.debug_reporter = orig_debug_reporter
+    end
+
     def test_request_calls_metrics_reporter
       orig_metrics_reporter = Autotuner.metrics_reporter

--- lib/autotuner/heuristic/oldmalloc.rb

@@ -39,7 +39,7 @@
 
       def call(request_context)
         # major_by is only useful if we ran at least one major GC during the request
-        if request_context.after_gc_context.stat[:major_gc_count] ==
+        if request_context.tuning_report.stat[:major_gc_count] ==
             request_context.before_gc_context.stat[:major_gc_count]
           return
         end

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?

 module Autotuner

-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?

-      @heuristics = Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
+      @heuristics = Autotuner.supported_heuristics.map { |h| h.new(nil) }
     end
 
     def test_heuristics
-      assert_operator(Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
+      assert_operator(Autotuner.supported_heuristics.length, :>, 0)
 
-      Autotuner.enabled_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?

problem_statement:
Allow heuristics to be disabled
We are experimenting with `autotuner`, and we've noticed that following the `GCCompact` suggestion doesn't have a meaningful impact on our app. 

I'm thinking of rolling back the changes and want to disable the check so it doesn't show up on our logs anymore (with `GCCompact.disable!` on a initializer), but the list of enabled heuristic is defined at load time and calling `disable!` won't have any effect.

Shouldn't the gem remove `Autotuner::Heuristics::ENABLED_HEURISTICS`, and build the array of heuristics after the app has booted?

-        assert_predicate(heuristic, :enabled?)
+      Autotuner.supported_heuristics.each do |heuristic|
+        assert_predicate(heuristic, :supported?)
       end
     end
